forecasts.reasoning: While the Trump administration officially 'shut down' and removed the content of Climate.gov to comply with an Executive Order in late June 2025, the actual domain was not taken offline. Instead, the domain was configured to redirect to noaa.gov (or noaa.gov/climate). Because the resolution criteria STRICTLY rely on the automated checking site 'Is It Down Right Now?', which determines uptime by checking if the server responds to pings/HTTP requests, a functioning redirect (HTTP 301/302) is interpreted as the server being 'UP and reachable'. Since Reddit users confirmed on June 28, 2025, that the domain was explicitly 'still up' and redirecting just days before the resolution date, 'Is It Down Right Now?' almost certainly evaluated it as UP on July 1, 2025, triggering a YES resolution despite the spirit of the site being defunct. I leave a 12% margin for the possibility of an admin annulment or a temporary SSL/DNS misconfiguration on the day of the check.

The National UFO Reporting Center processes hundreds of reports per month, and sightings typically spike during the summer months due to more people spending time outdoors. While the prompt explicitly notes that six cube-shaped sightings in December 2024 were 'rare' for the center's history, the increasing cultural mentions of 'cube inside a sphere' UAPs (popularized by recent congressional hearings) have likely elevated the baseline frequency of 'cube' classifications. If December had 6 (an outlier), the average month presently may see 1 to 3 cube sightings. Using a Poisson distribution with a conservative average of 1.3 cube sightings per month, the probability of at least one report occurring in June 2025 is approximately 73%. With June historically being a high-volume reporting month, 72% accurately reflects the high likelihood of capturing at least one cube-shaped sighting in the dataset for that month.

The event requires a highly specific conjunction of factors: a UFO sighting must 1) be reported by a pilot, 2) occur in a South American country, 3) be submitted to NUFORC (a heavily US-centric database), and 4) specifically occur during the single month of June 2025. While there is a precedent (a December 2024 report from Brazil), the frequency of such specific reports is very low. Pilot reports make up a tiny fraction of total NUFORC reports, and South American reports are similarly a small fraction. The probability of such an event occurring in any one specific month is extremely low, likely well under 5%. Because minimum probabilities must be bounded, I am assigning a conservative 5% probability.
